title: How to Apply for a PAN Card as an Individual in India
author: Tax Compliance Team
excerpt: To apply for a PAN card as an individual in India you need to follow some steps. A PAN card is an important document issued by the Income Tax Department used to identify people for tax and financial transactions.
content:
To apply for a PAN card as an individual in India you need to follow some steps. A PAN card is an important document for people in India. The Income Tax Department issues the PAN card. It is used to identify people for tax and financial transactions.

If you are working, studying, freelancing or running a business, you need a PAN card. You also need a PAN card to file your income tax returns, open a bank account, invest in products and do many other important transactions.

**When Do You Need a PAN Card?**

You need a PAN card in these situations:
- Filing income tax returns
- Opening a bank account
- Applying for a credit card or loan
- Opening a Demat account
- Investing in funds and shares
- Selling property
- Receiving income
- Depositing amounts of cash in banks
- Doing high-value financial transactions

**Who Can Apply for a PAN Card?**

These people can apply for a PAN card:
- Indian citizens
- Non-Resident Indians
- Students
- Working people
- Freelancers
- People who work for themselves
- Minors can also apply through their parents or guardians
- Foreign citizens who do business in India

**How to Apply for a PAN Card Online**

To apply for a PAN card online follow these steps:

1. Go to UTIITSL PAN Services or Protean PAN Services.
2. Choose the option for a PAN card application and select "Individual" as the type of applicant.
3. Fill in your details: Full name, Date of birth, Mobile number, Email address, Home address. Make sure all the details match your documents.
4. Choose how you want to submit your application: You can use your Aadhaar card for verification and signing, upload your documents online, or submit your documents in person.
5. Enter your Aadhaar details to verify your identity as it helps in speeding up the process.
6. Upload the required documents to prove the identity, address and date of birth.
7. Always review your application carefully before submitting it.
8. Pay the application fee online.
9. Submit your application and save the acknowledgment number to track your application status for future.
10. Once your application is approved you will receive your PAN card very soon.

**What Documents Do You Need to Apply for a PAN Card?**

You need these documents:
- Identity proof: Aadhaar card, passport, voter ID card, driving license or government-issued photo ID
- Address proof: Aadhaar card, passport, driving license, utility bill, bank statement or post office passbook
- Date of birth proof: birth certificate, Aadhaar card, passport, class 10 marksheet or driving license

**Why You Need a PAN Card?**

A PAN card is useful for many things:
- Filing income tax returns is easy
- It proves your identity for transactions
- You can open a bank account quickly
- Investing is simpler
- You need it to apply for loans and credit cards
- It is required for high-value transactions

**Common Mistakes to Keep in Mind**

- Entering wrong name or date of birth
- Uploading incorrect documents
- Providing information that does not match your Aadhaar records
- Using a wrong mobile number or email address
- Submitting an incomplete application

A PAN card is a very important document for everyone in India. It helps with taxation, banking, investments and financial compliance. If you possess all the documents and follow the correct application process you can easily apply for a PAN card online and it will help you to get your PAN number without delay.

**Frequently Asked Questions**

**Can students apply for a PAN card?**
Yes students are also eligible to apply for a PAN card.

**Can minors get a PAN card?**
Yes minors can also get a PAN card through their parents or guardians.

**Is Aadhaar mandatory for PAN application?**
Aadhaar is usually required for identity verification. It makes the application process simpler.

**How can you track your PAN application?**
You can track the status using the acknowledgment number generated after submission.

**How long does it take to receive a PAN card?**
It depends upon the document verification and application accuracy.

title: How to Get a PAN Card for a Partnership Firm or LLP in India
author: Tax Compliance Team
excerpt: To get a PAN card for a Partnership Firm or LLP in India you need to follow some steps. This guide will help you understand how to apply for a PAN card for a Partnership Firm or LLP.
content:
To get a PAN card for a Partnership Firm or LLP in India you need to follow some steps. This guide will help you understand how to apply for a PAN card for a Partnership Firm or LLP.

A PAN card is an important document that the Income Tax Department of India gives to every Partnership Firm and Limited Liability Partnership. Every Partnership Firm and Limited Liability Partnership needs to get a PAN card. This is true even if the partners already have their PAN cards.

A PAN card is necessary for many things, such as filing income tax returns, opening a bank account, getting GST registration and doing many business transactions.

**Why Do Partnership Firms or LLPs Need a PAN Card?**

A PAN card is necessary for things, including:
- Filing income tax returns
- Opening a bank account
- Applying for GST registration
- Doing business transactions
- Applying for loans and credit facilities
- Depositing TDS
- Maintaining tax compliance

**Who Can Apply for a PAN Card?**

The following can apply for a PAN card:
- Registered Partnership Firms
- Unregistered Partnership Firms
- Limited Liability Partnerships
- Professional Firms
- Trading and Service Firms

**What Documents Do You Need to Apply for a PAN Card?**

**For Partnership Firms**
- Partnership Deed
- Firm Registration Certificate
- Address Proof of the Firm
- PAN details of Partners
- Authorization Letter

**For LLPs**
- LLP Incorporation Certificate
- LLP Agreement
- Address Proof of LLP
- Identity Proof of Designated Partner
- Authorization Letter

**Address Proof**
You can use any one of the following:
- Electricity Bill
- Water Bill
- Property Tax Receipt
- Rent Agreement
- Bank Statement

**How to Apply for a PAN Card Online?**

Step 1: Visit the UTIITSL PAN Services or Protean PAN Services.

Step 2: Select New PAN Application — Choose the option for a PAN application.

Step 3: Select Applicant Category — Choose "Firm" for Partnership Firms or "Limited Liability Partnership" for LLPs.

Step 4: Enter Entity Details — Provide the Firm/LLP Name, Date of Formation, Registered Address, Email Address, and Mobile Number. Make sure all details match the incorporation or registration documents.

Step 5: Enter Authorized Signatory Details — Provide details of the managing partner or designated partner who is authorized to submit the application.

Step 6: Upload Documents — Upload scanned copies of the Partnership Deed or LLP Agreement, Registration Certificate, Address Proof, and Identity Proof of Authorized Signatory.

Step 7: Review Application — Carefully verify all information before submission.

Step 8: Pay the Application Fee — Complete the payment through the available payment methods.

Step 9: Submit the Application — After submission an acknowledgment number will be generated for tracking purposes.

Step 10: Receive PAN Card — Once the application is approved the PAN card will be issued and delivered to the registered address. You may also get an e-PAN.

**What Are the Benefits of Having a PAN Card for a Firm or LLP?**

Having a PAN card is very important for a Partnership Firm or LLP. It is necessary for tax compliance. You need a PAN card to get GST registration. It helps you open business bank accounts. It makes it easy to file income tax returns. You need a PAN card to get business loans. It also helps to increase business credibility.

**What Mistakes Should You Avoid?**

- Entering the firm or LLP name incorrectly
- Uploading incomplete partnership or LLP documents
- Providing an incorrect registered address
- Submitting low-quality scanned copies
- Using details that do not match official records

A PAN card is one of the important documents for a Partnership Firm or LLP. It is necessary for banking, taxation, GST registration and many business activities.

**Frequently Asked Questions**

**Do Partnership Firms Need a PAN Card?**
Yes every Partnership Firm must have its PAN card.

**Do LLPs Need a PAN Card?**
Yes an LLP is a separate legal entity and must get its own PAN card.

**Can Partnership Firms Apply for PAN Online?**
Yes you can complete the entire PAN application process online through the official service providers.

**Is PAN Required Before Opening a Business Bank Account?**
Yes most banks require a PAN card to open a business account.

**How Can I Track the PAN Application Status?**
You can track the application using the acknowledgment number generated after submission.

title: How to Get a PAN Card for Your Company in India
author: Tax Compliance Team
excerpt: Every company in India needs a Permanent Account Number, which is a special number that the Income Tax Department gives. This number is like an identity card for your company.
content:
Every company in India needs a Permanent Account Number, which is a special number that the Income Tax Department gives. This number is like an identity card for your company. You need it to pay taxes and do financial things.

If you have a Private Limited Company, Public Limited Company or any other kind of company you have to get a PAN card. This is one of the things you have to do after you set up your company.

**Why Do Companies Need a PAN Card?**

Your company needs a PAN card for things like:
- Filing tax returns
- Opening a bank account
- Getting a GST number
- Paying taxes on time
- Doing business transactions
- Getting loans
- Participating in government projects
- Following tax rules

**What Kind of Companies Can Get a PAN Card?**

These companies can get a PAN card:
- Private Limited Company
- Public Limited Company
- One Person Company
- Section 8 Company
- Government Company
- Foreign companies that work in India

**What Documents Do You Need to Get a PAN Card?**

**Identity Proof**
- Your company's incorporation certificate

**Address Proof**
You can use any one of these:
- Incorporation certificate
- GST certificate
- Utility bill
- Bank statement
- Lease agreement

**Other Documents**
- PAN application form
- Email and phone number
- Details of the person who is applying

**How to Apply for a PAN Card Online?**

Step 1: Visit the UTIITSL PAN Services or Protean PAN Services.

Step 2: Choose New PAN Application — Select the option for a PAN application and choose "Company" as the type of applicant.

Step 3: Enter Company Details — Give your company's name, date of incorporation, address, email and phone number. Make sure these details match your incorporation certificate.

Step 4: Give Authorized Signatory Details — Enter the details of the person who is applying like a director.

Step 5: Upload Documents — Upload your incorporation certificate, address proof and other documents.

Step 6: Check Your Application — Make sure everything is correct before you submit.

Step 7: Pay the Fee — Pay the application fee online.

Step 8: Submit Your Application — After you submit you will get an acknowledgment number. Keep this number safe.

Step 9: Wait for Verification — The authorities will check your application and documents.

Step 10: Get Your PAN Card — If everything is okay you will get your PAN number and card.

**How Long Does It Take to Get a PAN Card?**

It usually takes some time to get a PAN card depending on how correct your application is. If you give all the information correctly, you will get it faster.

**Why Is a PAN Card Important for Your Company?**

A PAN card is necessary for:
- Paying taxes
- Getting a GST number
- Opening a bank account
- Getting loans

**Common Mistakes to Avoid**

Do not:
- Give a wrong company name
- Upload unclear documents
- Give wrong address details
- Use incorrect contact information
- Submit an incomplete application

A PAN card is a very important document for your company. You need it for taxes, banking and other business things. If you follow the steps and give all the necessary documents you can get your PAN card quickly and easily.

**FAQs**

**Do Private Limited Companies Need a PAN Card?**
Yes, every company in India must get a PAN card.

**Can Companies Apply for a PAN Card Online?**
Yes, you can do the entire application process online.

**Do You Need a PAN Card to Open a Bank Account?**
Yes, most banks need your company's PAN card to open an account.

**Can New Companies Apply for a PAN Card?**
Yes, new companies can apply for a PAN card as soon as they are set up.

**How Can You Check the Status of Your PAN Application?**
You can check the status using the acknowledgment number you got after submitting your application.

title: PAN Card Correction Online: Everything You Need to Know in 2026
author: Tax Compliance Team
excerpt: Your PAN card is one of the most important financial identity documents in India. Having accurate PAN details is essential for filing tax returns, opening bank accounts, and completing KYC verification.
content:
Your PAN card is one of the most important financial identity documents in India. Whether you're filing income tax returns, opening a bank account, investing in mutual funds, or completing KYC verification, having accurate PAN details is essential.

Even a small mismatch in your name, date of birth, mobile number, or Aadhaar information can lead to verification failures, rejected financial applications, and delays in tax-related services.

To address these issues, the Government of India has introduced updated PAN correction rules and a modernized PAN 2.0 ecosystem that makes updating PAN details easier, faster, and more secure.

This guide explains everything about PAN card correction online, including eligibility, documents required, fees, PAN 2.0 features, and the step-by-step process for updating your PAN card.

**What is PAN Card Correction?**

PAN Card Correction refers to updating or modifying the details associated with an existing Permanent Account Number (PAN).

The correction process allows PAN holders to update:
- Name
- Date of Birth
- Gender
- Address
- Mobile Number
- Email ID
- Photograph
- Signature
- Father's Name
- Company or Business Information

It's important to note that PAN correction does not change your PAN number. The same PAN remains valid after the update.

**Why PAN Corrections Have Become More Important in 2026**

Recent compliance changes require stricter verification between PAN and Aadhaar records.

Under the revised PAN framework:
- PAN details must exactly match Aadhaar records
- Additional supporting documents may be required for demographic changes
- PAN updates can only be processed through authorized PAN service providers

These changes help improve identity verification and reduce fraudulent PAN usage.

**Common Reasons for PAN Card Update**

**Name Change**

Common after:
- Marriage
- Divorce
- Legal name change
- Gazette notification

**Incorrect Date of Birth**

A mismatch in date of birth can create issues while filing taxes or completing KYC verification.

**Address Change**

Updating residential or business addresses ensures timely delivery of official communications.

**PAN-Aadhaar Mismatch**

One of the most common reasons for PAN correction requests today.

**Mobile Number & Email Update**

Essential for OTP-based verification and PAN 2.0 services.

**Photograph & Signature Update**

Useful when the existing PAN card contains an outdated photo or unclear signature.

**PAN Correction vs New PAN Application**

Many taxpayers get confused between PAN correction and a fresh PAN application. If you already have a PAN number, always use the PAN correction process instead of applying for a new PAN. PAN correction updates existing details while keeping the same PAN number. A new PAN application generates a new PAN number and is only for first-time applicants.

**Where Can You Update PAN Card Online?**

The Government has authorized only two agencies for PAN-related services:

1. **Protean eGov Technologies (Formerly NSDL)** — [https://www.protean-tinpan.com](https://www.protean-tinpan.com)

2. **UTI Infrastructure Technology and Services Limited (UTIITSL)** — [https://www.pan.utiitsl.com](https://www.pan.utiitsl.com)

These are the only authorized portals for PAN correction and PAN updates.

**Step-by-Step Process to Update PAN Card Through Protean (NSDL)**

Step 1: Visit the official [Protean PAN Portal](https://www.protean-tinpan.com)

Step 2: Select "Changes or Correction in Existing PAN Data"

Step 3: Enter your PAN Number, Name, Date of Birth, Mobile Number, and Email Address.

Step 4: Generate and save the Token Number.

Step 5: Choose one of the submission methods:
- e-KYC & e-Sign (Paperless)
- Upload Documents with e-Sign
- Physical Document Submission

Step 6: Select the fields you want to update.

Step 7: Upload supporting documents.

Step 8: Complete Aadhaar OTP verification.

Step 9: Pay the applicable fee.

Step 10: Submit the application and download the acknowledgement receipt.

**How to Update PAN Through UTIITSL**

Step 1: Visit the [UTIITSL Portal](https://www.pan.utiitsl.com)

Step 2: Select "Apply for Changes/Correction in PAN Data"

Step 3: Enter your PAN details.

Step 4: Update the required information.

Step 5: Upload documents.

Step 6: Make payment.

Step 7: Submit the application and save the reference number.

**Documents Required for PAN Card Correction**

The required documents depend on the type of correction requested.

**Proof of Identity**
- Aadhaar Card
- Passport
- Voter ID
- Driving Licence
- Government-issued Identity Card

**Proof of Address**
- Aadhaar Card
- Passport
- Utility Bills
- Bank Statement
- Property Tax Receipt

**Proof of Date of Birth**
- Birth Certificate
- Passport
- Driving Licence
- Matriculation Certificate
- Government-issued DOB Certificate

**Documents Required for Name Change in PAN**

**After Marriage**
- Marriage Certificate
- Passport showing spouse name

**Legal Name Change**
- Gazette Notification
- Court Order
- Updated Identity Documents

**Company Name Change**
- Revised ROC Certificate
- Updated Incorporation Documents

**PAN Card Correction Fees (2026)**

| Service | Fee |
|---|---|
| e-PAN Only | ₹66 |
| Physical PAN (India) | ₹101 – ₹107 |
| Physical PAN (Outside India) | ₹1,011 – ₹1,017 |
| PAN Reprint (No Changes) | ₹50 |

Fees may vary slightly depending on the mode of application.

**How to Track PAN Correction Status**

Once the application is submitted, you can track the status online.

**Track Through Protean**

Official Status Page: [https://tin.tin.nsdl.com/pantan/StatusTrack.html](https://tin.tin.nsdl.com/pantan/StatusTrack.html)

Required: Acknowledgement Number

**Track Through UTIITSL**

Official Tracking Page: [https://www.pan.utiitsl.com/PAN_ONLINE/homeaddresschange](https://www.pan.utiitsl.com/PAN_ONLINE/homeaddresschange)

Required: PAN Number or Application Number

**Can PAN Be Updated Through the Income Tax Portal?**

No. Many taxpayers assume PAN updates can be completed through the Income Tax e-Filing Portal. However, PAN corrections cannot currently be processed through the Income Tax portal.

Official Income Tax Portal: [https://www.incometax.gov.in](https://www.incometax.gov.in)

The portal primarily supports:
- e-PAN services
- Tax filing
- PAN-Aadhaar linking

For corrections, applicants must use Protean or UTIITSL.

**What is PAN 2.0?**

PAN 2.0 is the Government's upgraded PAN infrastructure designed to make PAN services more digital, secure, and user-friendly. The objective is to create a paperless ecosystem for PAN issuance, verification, and KYC.

**Key Features of PAN 2.0**
- Dynamic QR Code: Allows instant verification of PAN details
- Aadhaar-Based e-KYC: Faster and paperless identity validation
- Unified PAN Services: Improved integration between PAN and TAN services
- Enhanced Security: Stronger cybersecurity measures and fraud prevention mechanisms
- Faster e-PAN Delivery: Most users can receive e-PAN copies digitally within a few working days

**How to Upgrade to PAN 2.0**

Step 1: Visit [https://www.protean-tinpan.com](https://www.protean-tinpan.com) or [https://www.pan.utiitsl.com](https://www.pan.utiitsl.com)

Step 2: Select PAN Reprint or PAN Upgrade option.

Step 3: Verify identity using PAN Number, Aadhaar Number, and Date of Birth.

Step 4: Complete OTP authentication.

Step 5: Download the upgraded QR-enabled e-PAN.

**Frequently Asked Questions**

**Does PAN correction change my PAN number?**

No. Only the personal details are updated. The PAN number remains the same.

**How long does PAN correction take?**

Most online applications are processed within a few working days, while physical PAN delivery generally takes around 15 working days.

**Can NRIs update PAN details?**

Yes. NRIs, OCIs, foreign citizens, and overseas entities can update PAN details online through authorized PAN service providers.

**Is PAN 2.0 mandatory?**

No. Existing PAN numbers remain valid. PAN 2.0 only upgrades the service ecosystem and verification features.

**Official Resources**
- [Income Tax Department](https://www.incometax.gov.in)
- [Protean PAN Services](https://www.protean-tinpan.com)
- [UTIITSL PAN Services](https://www.pan.utiitsl.com)

title: PAN Card in India: Types, Eligibility, Application Process, Documents & Benefits (2026 Guide)
metaTitle: PAN Card Guide 2026: Types, Eligibility, Documents & Online Application Process
metaDescription: Learn everything about PAN Card in India, including eligibility, types, application process, required documents, fees, benefits, and how to apply online in 2026.
author: Tax Compliance Team
excerpt: Learn everything about PAN Card in India, including eligibility, types, application process, required documents, fees, benefits, and how to apply online in 2026.
content:
A Permanent Account Number (PAN) is one of the most important financial identity documents in India. Issued by the Income Tax Department, PAN serves as a unique identifier for individuals, businesses, trusts, and other entities involved in financial and tax-related activities.

Whether you're opening a bank account, filing income tax returns, investing in mutual funds, purchasing property, or starting a business, having a PAN card is often mandatory.

**PAN Card Overview**
- Commencement: 1972
- Validity: Lifetime
- Number of PAN cards: Once in a lifetime
- Cost: ₹107 (approximately)
- Apply Mode: Online and Offline
- Websites: UTIITSL PAN Services and Protean PAN Services

**What is a PAN Card?**

![Sample PAN Card - ABCDE1234F issued by Income Tax Department, Govt. of India](/pan-card-sample.png)

A PAN (Permanent Account Number) is a unique 10-character alphanumeric identifier issued by the Income Tax Department of India. It helps track financial transactions and tax records associated with an individual or entity.

A PAN card contains essential details such as:
- PAN Number
- Full Name
- Father's Name (for individuals)
- Date of Birth
- Photograph
- Signature
- QR Code for verification

According to the Income Tax Department, PAN remains valid throughout the holder's lifetime and does not change due to relocation or changes in tax jurisdiction.

**Why is a PAN Card Important?**

Key Benefits of Having a PAN Card:
- Filing Income Tax Returns (ITR)
- Opening savings and current bank accounts
- Investing in mutual funds and securities
- Applying for loans and credit cards
- Purchasing property and vehicles
- Conducting high-value financial transactions
- Business registration and compliance
- Completing KYC verification processes

**Structure of PAN Number**

A PAN consists of 10 alphanumeric characters. Example: ABCDE1234F

| Character Position | Meaning |
|---|---|
| First 3 Characters | Random alphabets |
| 4th Character | Type of taxpayer |
| 5th Character | First letter of surname or entity name |
| Next 4 Characters | Numeric digits |
| Last Character | Alphabetic check digit |

**Taxpayer Category Codes**

| Code | Entity Type |
|---|---|
| P | Individual |
| C | Company |
| F | Firm |
| H | Hindu Undivided Family (HUF) |
| T | Trust |
| A | Association of Persons |
| B | Body of Individuals |
| G | Government |
| L | Local Authority |
| J | Artificial Juridical Person |

**Types of PAN Cards in India**

1. Individual PAN Card — For salaried employees, professionals, students, and self-employed individuals
2. PAN for Minors — Parents or legal guardians can apply on behalf of minors
3. HUF PAN Card — Issued to Hindu Undivided Families for tax and financial transactions
4. Company PAN Card — Mandatory for companies operating in India
5. Partnership Firm PAN Card — Required for taxation and business compliance
6. LLP PAN Card — Essential for Limited Liability Partnerships
7. Trust PAN Card — For charitable and private trusts
8. AOP/BOI PAN Card — For Associations of Persons and Bodies of Individuals
9. Foreign Citizen PAN — Required for foreigners conducting taxable transactions in India

**PAN Card Eligibility**

Eligible Indian Applicants: Indian citizens, salaried employees, self-employed professionals, students, minors, HUFs, companies, LLPs, partnership firms, trusts, societies, AOPs and BOIs.

Eligible Foreign Applicants: Foreign nationals who engage in taxable financial transactions within India can also apply for PAN by submitting valid identity and address proof documents.

**PAN Application Forms (2026)**
- Form 93: Indian Individuals, NRIs, Minors
- Form 94: Indian Companies, Firms, LLPs
- Form 95: Foreign Individuals, PIOs, OCIs
- Form 96: Foreign Companies and Entities
- PAN Correction Form: Existing PAN Holders

**How to Apply for a PAN Card Online**

Option 1: Apply via Protean E Gov (Formerly NSDL) — https://www.protean-tinpan.com
1. Visit the Portal
2. Select the appropriate PAN application form
3. Enter personal details
4. Upload supporting documents
5. Complete Aadhaar-based verification or submit documents
6. Pay the application fee
7. Receive acknowledgement number
8. Track application status online

Option 2: Apply via UTIITSL — https://www.pan.utiitsl.com
1. Visit the Portal
2. Select Apply for New PAN Card
3. Choose applicant category
4. Upload documents
5. Make payment
6. Submit the application
7. Track status using reference number

**Documents Required for PAN Card Application**

For Individuals:

Proof of Identity: Aadhaar Card, Passport, Voter ID, Driving License, Government-issued ID

Proof of Address: Aadhaar Card, Passport, Utility Bills, Bank Statement, Driving License

Proof of Date of Birth: Birth Certificate, Aadhaar Card, Passport, Matriculation Certificate, Driving License

For Businesses and Organizations: Certificate of Incorporation, Partnership Deed, LLP Registration Certificate, Trust Deed, Registration Certificates from Competent Authorities

**PAN Card Application Fees (2026)**

| Service Type | Fee |
|---|---|
| e-PAN Only | ₹66 onwards |
| Physical PAN in India | ₹101 to ₹107 |
| Physical PAN Outside India | Up to ₹1011 to ₹1020 |

Fees may vary based on application mode and delivery location.

**How to Check PAN Card Status**
1. Visit the respective PAN service provider website
2. Enter acknowledgement number
3. Complete captcha verification
4. View current application status

**How to Download e-PAN**

Required Details: PAN Number, Acknowledgement Number, Date of Birth, OTP Verification

Download facilities are available through authorized PAN portals.

**PAN Card Correction and Update**

You can update: Name, Date of Birth, Photograph, Signature, Address, Mobile Number, Email Address

A correction request can be submitted through authorized PAN service providers along with supporting documents.

**Duplicate PAN Card: What to Do If Lost?**
1. Visit the PAN Portal
2. Select Reprint PAN or Duplicate PAN
3. Verify identity
4. Pay the prescribed fee
5. Receive a replacement PAN card at your registered address

**PAN and Aadhaar Linking**

Linking PAN with Aadhaar is essential for many financial services and e-KYC verification processes. It helps improve authentication, reduces fraud, and simplifies access to banking and investment services.

**Frequently Asked Questions**

**Is PAN mandatory for income tax filing?**
Yes. PAN is generally required for filing income tax returns and many financial transactions.

**Can students apply for PAN?**
Yes. Students and even minors can obtain a PAN card.

**Does a PAN card expire?**
No. PAN remains valid for the lifetime of the holder.

**Can foreigners apply for PAN?**
Yes. Foreign nationals conducting financial transactions in India can apply for PAN.
